key_insight: CORAL uses reinforcement learning to better dock small molecules onto amyloid fibrils by rewarding both protein-ligand binding and cooperative stacking between neighboring ligands.
clinical_relevance: This could improve design of amyloid-targeting imaging probes, diagnostics, and potentially therapeutics for neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer’s and Parkinson’s.
method: Generative docking model trained with reinforcement learning
Reward combines docking affinity with cooperative ligand-ligand stacking energy
Tailored to cross-β groove geometry of amyloid fibrils
evidence_signal: The authors report improved pose quality and stronger binding-affinity correlation on experimentally resolved structures and on a curated amyloid-ligand evaluation set built from expert-validated model-generated poses.
watch_item: Because true amyloid-ligand co-crystal structures are scarce, performance may still depend on limited, curated evaluation data and may not generalize broadly without further experimental validation.
paper_title: CORAL: Learning Amyloid Fibril Ligand Docking with Cooperative Binding Rewards
